page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DADSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 1843 GB, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPU is included.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core compute capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Cryptographic performance is also strong, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instructions. Multi-threaded decompression and image processing workloads demonstrate top-tier performance. However, memory bandwidth benchmarks for both read and write operations fall within the average range. Large-scale LLM inference, such as text generation with a 70B parameter model, is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

The D48ads_v5 offers a balanced resource density, combining substantial CPU resources with a large local storage allocation. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring local scratch space without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The hardware profile is suited for relational databases, multi-threaded data processing, web serving, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.525 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic execution, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks. However, memory bandwidth and single-core operations remain in the average tier, and large-scale LLM text generation is a performance bottleneck. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage, the D48ads_v5 provides qualitative resource density for multi-threaded workloads. It is suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring AMD EPYC processors, substantial local storage, and strong multi-core processing capabilities for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
Equipped with 192 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local NVMe storage
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average memory bandwidth and single-core processing speeds
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
